summary: Iteration 3 testing of ZenStay new features (host contact, file upload + serving, email confirmation hook, filters, DateRangePicker, ImageUpload). Backend: 27/27 pytest tests pass (16 iter1+2 regression + 11 new iter3). Frontend: filters, host form, DateRangePicker on listing detail all verified e2e against the public preview URL. No CORS errors. Admin/ImageUpload visual flow could not be browser-tested (httpOnly+secure session cookie cannot be injected from headless browser without going through Google Auth), but the underlying /api/admin/upload endpoint is fully covered by pytest including auth/forbidden/oversize/non-image rejections and round-trip serve via /api/files/{path}.
action_items: Optional: Convert `return rid` to `assert` in test_reservation_create_and_list (PytestReturnNotNoneWarning — cosmetic).
Optional: Migrate FastAPI @app.on_event handlers to lifespan (deprecation warning).
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/host-contact correctly persists then fires the Make webhook best-effort inside try/except — webhook failure does not break the response. Good.

send_booking_confirmation is awaited inside both /api/payments/status (poll path) and /api/webhook/stripe — invoked exactly when payment_status_val == 'paid' AND prior status != 'paid', so idempotent. Resend errors are swallowed and logged. Good.

/api/admin/upload enforces content_type allowlist BEFORE reading body, but file size is checked AFTER reading the full body — a malicious admin could still cause memory spike with very large uploads beyond 8MB. Acceptable for an admin-only endpoint, but consider switching to UploadFile.file streaming + size budget if you ever loosen auth.

/api/files/{path:path} relies on db.files.find_one to gate access (good — prevents arbitrary storage path enumeration). Public, no auth required — intended for serving listing images. Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600 is reasonable.
Object storage init_storage uses a module-level mutable cache (_storage_key). Not concurrency-safe under uvicorn workers >1; today single-worker so OK. Worth a lock if scaled.
DateRangePicker onSelect closes popover only when both from+to selected — good UX. Disabled past dates. shadcn Calendar used (no native browser date picker). Good.
Frontend AuthContext now uses per-call withCredentials (verified via no CORS preflight failures during browser session). Listings request 200 OK from preview URL.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (listings filters, host form, DateRangePicker). Admin/ImageUpload visual flow not browser-tested due to session-cookie limitation; backend endpoint fully tested.

test_credentials: admin@zenstay.com / alice@zenstay.com via seeded user_sessions tokens test_admin_session_zs (iter1+2) and test_admin_session_zs3 / test_user_session_zs3 (iter3). Both fixtures auto-seed and auto-cleanup.
seed_data_creation: iter3 test creates one TEST_ host_contact record (cleaned up at module teardown by email TEST_ prefix) and one image file in db.files + object storage (not cleaned — small ~84B PNG, negligible).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two pytest files now cover the backend: test_zenstay.py (16) and test_zenstay_iter3.py (11). Run both with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/results.xml`. The iter3 file uses distinct session tokens (test_admin_session_zs3 / test_user_session_zs3) so it can run in parallel with iter1+2 without collision. Object storage uses EMERGENT_LLM_KEY — verified the init→put→get round-trip works against integrations.emergentagent.com. Resend email is wired but only fires when Stripe payment becomes 'paid' (cannot reach in test mode); the helper itself was unit-tested and does not raise. For frontend admin testing in future iterations, you'll need to either (a) implement a backend test-only login route to set the session_token cookie, or (b) perform real Google OAuth flow — currently the httpOnly+secure cookie prevents headless injection.
